title: podman push --authfile failed with auth error even after podman login (aws ecr
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I am building my nodejs application container using buildah bud command, my plan is to build oci format container image and push it to ecr repo, so that i can run ecs fargate containers using that oci formatted image.

but the problem is, everything working until podman push. when i push image to ecr with podman push, i am getting auth error as below,

```
Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:1a058d5342cc722ad5439cacae4b2b4eedde51d8fe8800fcf28444302355c16d at destination: checking whether a blob sha256:1a058d5342cc722ad5439cacae4b2b4eedde51d8fe8800fcf28444302355c16d exists in myrepo.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/my-task unauthorized: authentication required
```

i have tried with --authfile option as well, but its that too giving me same error. i can see that auth.json has valid credentials by cat that file.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. getting aws ecr password using aws cli command `aws ecr get-login-password`

2. login in using `podman login` command (password from above command using `--password-stdin`) and saving the auth.json into a specific directory

3. podman push with `--authfile=<path to auth.json>` saved by the above command.

**Describe the results you received:**

when I run the above commands using a custom script .sh file, I am getting auth error as given below

```
Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:1a058d5342cc722ad5439cacae4b2b4eedde51d8fe8800fcf28444302355c16d at destination: checking whether a blob sha256:1a058d5342cc722ad5439cacae4b2b4eedde51d8fe8800fcf28444302355c16d exists in myrepo.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/my-task unauthorized: authentication required
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

it should push my local container Image to the given ecr repo as podman login is already successful and have auth.json. but it says auth error - unauthorized. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

podman push always failed with error auth error. nothing else.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 05:30:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```aws-cli/2.4.5 Python/3.8.8 Linux/5.10.60.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 exe/x86_64.ubuntu.20 prompt/off```

for security reasons, I just changed the repo name and image name, but everything else is the same.

please help me to get rid of this issue. it's really blocking my productivity.

thanks in advance!

comments.body:
Thank you for reaching out. The debug logs show the following line

> DEBU[0000] No credentials for my-pvt-ecr-repo.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com found

Please check if you logged into this registry (i.e., `my-pvt-ecr-repo.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com`) and that the specified authfile includes credentials for this specific registry.

Hi @vrothberg ,

Thanks for your quick response,

Yeah, I got it. 

Actually, i made a mistake in the repo URL while pushing, so it's failed to push there.  after correcting the repo URL, podman push successfully pushed my image to the ecr repo.

Thanks again!
Awesome, glad the issue has been resolved!
